title: ESPHome Device Builder Board Definition
description: Schema for board manifest.yaml files in the ESPHome Device Builder.

id.description: Unique board identifier. Must match the folder name.

esphome.description: ESPHome platform configuration for this board.

variant.description: ESP32 chip variant. Only for esp32 platform.

framework.description: Build framework override. Omit or null for platform default.

images.description: Board images. URLs or paths relative to the manifest. First is primary.

tags.description: Unique feature tags not captured by platform/variant/connectivity.

docs_url.description: Link to ESPHome documentation for this platform.

featured.description: Highlighted board (e.g. official ESPHome starter kits).

is_generic.description: True for generic fallback boards where not all pins may be physically accessible.

featured_components.description: Components recommended for this board. Surfaced as a 'Recommended' section in the Add Component dialog.

featured_bundles.description: Logical groups of featured components added together (hardware addon modules, status-LED setups, ...).

default_components.description: Components installed by default in every new device on this board. Each entry resolves through a two-step lookup, first as a local featured_components.id (picks up that entry's full field presets), then as a bare catalog component_id (no presets). The object form supplements presets with inline 'fields:' overrides (e.g. 'web_server' with 'version: 3').

source.description: Marks the manifest as imported from an external catalog. Hand-curated boards omit this block; the sync script regenerates manifests where it's present and never touches manifests where it's absent.

type.description: Origin marker. Drives which sync script owns this manifest.

remote_id.description: Upstream identifier (folder name, page slug, ...) used to match this manifest back to its source on subsequent syncs.

upstream_url.description: Direct link to the source page on the upstream repo or site.

upstream_revision.description: Commit SHA (or version string) the manifest was generated from.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 of the upstream source content. Lets the sync skip already-up-to-date entries without re-rendering.

pin.description: A GPIO pin on the board.

label.description: Silkscreen label on the physical board (e.g. 'D1', 'GPIO0', 'A0').

available.description: true = exposed on headers, false = not broken out, null = unknown (generic boards).

occupied_by.description: Onboard component using this pin (e.g. 'Built-in LED', 'SPI Flash', 'BOOT button').

featured_component.description: A component recommended for this board, optionally with pre-filled field values.

id.description: Local id, unique within this board (e.g. 'relay', 'pir_motion'). Lowercase letters / digits / underscores; must not equal the domain of component_id.

name.description: Override for the component's display name.

featured_bundle.description: A logical group of featured components added together.

id.description: Local id, unique within this board. Lowercase letters / digits / underscores.

description.description: What the bundle represents (e.g. 'Onboard status LED').

component_ids.description: Local ids referring to entries in featured_components on this same board.

default_component.description: Either a bare ref (string shorthand for { id: <ref> }) or an object with optional inline field presets.

fields.description: Plain key: value overrides applied on top of any featured-component presets.

field_preset_or_value.description: Either a primitive shorthand (pre-fills a default), a list, or a verbose object form ({value, locked, suggestions}).

value.description: Pre-filled value. Optional when only suggestions are set.

locked.description: When true, frontend disables the input and the backend rejects deviating user input on add. Requires 'value'.

suggestions.description: Limit the user's choice to this list (e.g. allowed GPIOs).
